title: identity — trait composition
description: Two owned traits. `IsIdentity` is a marker composed by the Identity model itself and any future variant (e.g. a shadow Identity used during merge flows). `HasCredentialLifecycle` provides the composable audit/reset/lockout methods that both `Identity` and the downstream principals which reuse Identity substrate (`PlatformUser`) need. NO BelongsToTenant — Identity is global by design.

owned.description: Marker trait composed by the Identity model. Provides the shared shape identifier for reflection-based tooling (audit redactors, seeders, factories) that need to enumerate every Identity-shaped row without hard-coding the class. Adds no accessor / mutator / scope of its own — those live on HasCredentialLifecycle.

Composable audit/reset/lockout methods for principals whose credentials live on the `identities` table. Ships: `->verifyPassword($plain)`, `->markLoginSuccess()`, `->markLoginFailure()`, `->lock($minutes = null)`, `->unlock()`, `->enrollMfa($secret)`, `->disableMfa()`, `->regenerateRecoveryCodes()`, `->consumeRecoveryCode($code)`, `->recordPasswordChange($newHash)`, `->requiresEmailVerification()`. Each method wraps the operation in a transaction, fires the matching event, and writes to the audit trail via `HasActivityLog` when composed alongside it.
owned.wraps: The trait DELEGATES to Laravel's Hash + Password facades for credential operations but wraps them in idempotent domain operations (progressive lockout, breach-check integration, history maintenance). We do not reimplement password hashing — we compose it.

framework.reason: Identity retains a GDPR erasure hold-period (default 30 days after deleted_at) before hard delete so downstream compliance queries can still resolve historical audit trails.

notes: NO BelongsToTenant — Identity is global (design.md D1).
NO application-scope trait — Identity spans every Application via the identity_id FK on `users` / `platform_users`.
The order matters: `IsIdentity` must precede `HasCredentialLifecycle` so the marker trait's static hooks run before the lifecycle trait's booted() observer wiring.
`HasActivityLog` writes to the shared `activity_log` table so identity mutations show up in the compliance timeline; PII fields are redacted at the audit layer (see data-classes.json).
